title: ollama/ollama
summary: Official CLI and server for running large language models locally (Llama 2, Mistral, Gemma, etc.) with a simple API and Docker support.

title: ollama-js
summary: Official JavaScript/TypeScript client for Ollama, published on npm as ‘ollama’.

title: ollama-python
summary: Official Python client for Ollama, available on PyPI as ‘ollama’.

title: langchain-ollama
summary: LangChain community integration that adds Ollama models as LLM and chat components.

title: ollama-webui
summary: Feature-rich web UI for Ollama (chat, model management, multi-user, dark mode).

title: ollama-cli
summary: Unofficial interactive CLI tool with chat history and prompt templates.

title: ollama-copilot
summary: VS Code extension that brings Ollama models into the editor as a GitHub-Copilot-like assistant.

title: ollama-discord
summary: Discord bot that lets servers chat with local Ollama models via slash commands.

title: ollama-slack
summary: Slack bot integration for Ollama, supporting threads and app mentions.

title: ollama-helm
summary: Community-maintained Helm chart for deploying Ollama on Kubernetes.

title: ollama-dagger
summary: Dagger module that wraps Ollama for CI/CD pipelines and reproducible model serving.

title: ollama-rag
summary: Minimal retrieval-augmented-generation example using Ollama + ChromaDB.

title: ollama4j
summary: Java/Kotlin client library for Ollama, published to Maven Central.

title: ollama-express
summary: Express.js starter that exposes REST endpoints powered by Ollama models.

title: ollama-streamlit
summary: Streamlit chat app that talks to Ollama, featuring markdown and code highlighting.

title: ollama-obsidian
summary: Obsidian plugin that lets users query local Ollama models from within notes.

title: ollama-nim
summary: Nim language client for Ollama with async HTTP and std/json support.

title: ollama-rust
summary: Rust crate providing strongly-typed bindings to the Ollama API.

title: ollama-elixir
summary: Elixir library wrapping Ollama’s REST API with GenServer-backed clients.
